title: thebasics-handbook-title-character-sheets
text: Character sheets hold your roleplay identity. Server owners can configure fields, required values, visibility, and which fields appear in /look. Player commands - /charsheet or /sheet: open your editable sheet. - /charsheet <player>: view another online player's visible sheet fields. - /bio: quick view alias for character sheets. - /look or /look <player>: inspect a nearby player. Without a name, it uses the player you are targeting. - /charsheetfields: list configured sheet fields and visibility. - /setcharsheet <field> <value>: set a field from chat. Aliases include /setsheet and /setbio. - /clearcharsheet [field]: clear one field, or all editable fields when no field is given. Aliases include /clearsheet and /clearbio. /look rules /look is meant for in-character inspection. By default the target must be online, nearby, and in line of sight. Self-look opens your full editable sheet. Fields marked self-only or admin-only are hidden from other players. Names and nicknames - /nick [name]: view or set your roleplay nickname when enabled. - /clearnick: clear your nickname. - /nickcolor [color] and /clearnickcolor: manage nickname color when enabled. If character sheets are enabled, nickname commands update the configured nickname sheet field. The display name usually prefers Full Name (Nickname), then Full Name, then Nickname, then account name. Headshots When enabled, your editable sheet can accept a PNG/JPEG drop for a character portrait. Some servers also allow a direct image URL. /clearheadshot removes your portrait. Character slots If RP character slots are enabled, use /character list, /character current, /character create <name>, /character select <id-or-name>, /character rename <id> <new name>, and /character archive <id-or-name>. Slots can save and restore sheet data, appearance, inventory, language state, chat preferences, health, hunger, position, spawn, and related roleplay state. Switching can be blocked while dead, carrying a cursor item, using an external container, or holding crafting-grid input items.
